name: La Vall d'en Bas – circular por Riudaura y El Mallol desde Sant Privat d'en Bas
region_intro: Hiking around La Vall D'En Bas offers diverse terrain within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region features significant mountain formations, including Puigsacalm (1,515 meters) and the Serra del Corb, alongside the cliffs of Aiats and Cabrera. Extensive forests of beech, oak, and cork oak trees cover much of the landscape, notably in areas like La Grevolosa. The valley also encompasses the fertile Bas plain, crisscrossed by streams that feed into the Fluvià river, and is home to waterfalls such as Salt de Sallent. Best hiking trails around La Vall D'En Bas The most popular hiking route is Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to notable peaks offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mina dels Bandolers and Salto de "l'Olla" - Circular from Els Hostalets d'en Bas, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route features a waterfall and passes through varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Forest of La Grevolosa - circular, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through dense beech, oak, and cork oak woodlands, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Hiking around La Vall D'En Bas is defined by mountain formations, dense forests, and impressive waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls through woodlands to more challenging ascents. The routes in La Vall D'En Bas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 640 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Vall D'En Bas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the different difficulty levels of hiking trails available in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: La Vall D'En Bas offers a wide range of hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. You can find approximately 13 easy routes, 52 moderate routes, and 30 more challenging trails, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: The terrain in La Vall D'En Bas is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter significant mountain formations like Puigsacalm and the Serra del Corb, dense forests of beech, oak, and cork oak, and the fertile Bas plain crisscrossed by streams. Some trails also feature steep ridges and cliffs, offering varied landscapes throughout your hike.

Q: Are there any impressive waterfalls to visit while hiking in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: Yes, the region is known for its spectacular waterfalls. A popular destination is the &lt;strong&gt;Salt de Sallent&lt;/strong&gt;. You can explore this natural wonder on routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938412563/salto-de-sallent-de-sant-privat-d-en-bas-circular-from-pla-d-en-xurri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Sallent de Sant Privat d'en Bas - Circular from Pla d'en Xurri&lt;/a&gt;. Another trail, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938310507/mina-dels-bandolers-and-salto-de-l-olla-circular-from-els-hostalets-d-en-bas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina dels Bandolers and Salto de "l'Olla" - Circular from Els Hostalets d'en Bas&lt;/a&gt;, also features a notable waterfall.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: For magnificent panoramic views, consider trails that ascend to higher elevations. The summit of &lt;strong&gt;Puigsacalm&lt;/strong&gt; (1,515 meters) and the &lt;strong&gt;Santa Magdalena massif&lt;/strong&gt; are renowned for offering expansive vistas across much of Catalonia, including the Pyrenees, Montseny, and even Montserrat. A route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926174269/puigsacalm-and-puig-dels-llops-circular-from-collado-de-bracons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons&lt;/a&gt; provides such opportunities.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: Yes, La Vall D'En Bas offers several easy strolls suitable for families. Trails through the dense woodlands, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976978719/forest-of-la-grevolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa - circular&lt;/a&gt;, are generally less demanding and provide a pleasant experience for all ages amidst nature.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in La Vall D'En Bas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976978719/forest-of-la-grevolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa - circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944403529/santuari-de-cabrera-y-sant-julia-de-cabrera-circular-by-the-aiats-cliffs" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de Cabrera y Sant Julià de Cabrera - Circular by the Aiats cliffs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 640 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's spectacular natural beauty, the diversity of its landscapes ranging from lush forests to mountain peaks, and the impressive waterfalls. The well-maintained trails and charming villages also receive positive feedback.

Q: Are there any interesting historical sites or charming villages to explore along the trails?
A: Yes, the valley is dotted with picturesque villages like Hostalets d'en Bas, Sant Esteve d'en Bas, Joanetes, Puigpardines, and El Mallol, many of which serve as starting points for hikes. You can also find historical paths, such as the old 'royal road' that once connected Olot with Vic, leading to medieval sites like Sant Miquel de Castelló.

Q: Are there any caves or unique natural monuments in the vicinity of the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around La Vall D'En Bas features several interesting geological formations. You might encounter caves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baumes de Caixurma&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Pixarelles&lt;/a&gt;. Another unique natural monument is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/741468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bufadors de Beví&lt;/a&gt;, which are natural rock formations with air currents.

Q: Is it possible to find trails that pass through dense forests?
A: La Vall D'En Bas is renowned for its extensive and dense forests. The &lt;strong&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa&lt;/strong&gt; is a prime example, showcasing ancient beech, oak, and cork oak trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976978719/forest-of-la-grevolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa - circular&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent way to immerse yourself in these woodlands.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Vall D'En Bas?
A: While hiking is enjoyable year-round, spring and autumn are often considered the best times. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers spectacular foliage colors in the extensive forests. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, and winters can be crisp but beautiful, especially at lower elevations.
tips: The Romanesque church of Santa Maria de Puigpardines, built in the 12th century, was part of the old monastery.
Viewpoint with an excellent panoramic view of the town of Olot and the volcanic area of La Garrotxa.
In the middle of a cycle track (if it has not rained, enough slippery rocks if there is humidity) with slopes of 8-9% between Ridaura and the road of Sant privat and Vidrà (by Coll Fred)
